prompt:
While researching a 3D-printed housing project, a student took the following notes:
• A printer builds each wall in under 24 hours.
• Material costs are roughly half those of brick.
• The first ten homes were occupied this year.

The student wants to emphasize speed and cost. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Using a large printer, crews are able to build each individual wall of a house in well under twenty-four hours.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize speed and cost as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The very first ten homes built by the project were finished and occupied by families earlier this year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize speed and cost as the prompt asks.

choices.text: 3D printing, which forms objects by adding material layer upon layer, is now being applied to houses as well.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize speed and cost as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Printing each wall in under a day and at roughly half the material cost of brick, the project delivered its first ten homes this year.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize speed and cost.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize speed and cost; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a salt marsh carbon study, a student took the following notes:
• Marsh mud stores carbon for centuries.
• An acre traps as much carbon as a forest.
• Draining the marsh releases it all at once.

The student wants to stress the marsh's value for storing carbon. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: When a salt marsh is drained for development, the carbon it once held is released back into the air all at once.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the marsh's value for storing carbon as the prompt asks.

choices.text: An acre of salt marsh can trap as much carbon as a forest, locking it away in mud for centuries unless the marsh is drained.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to stress the marsh's value for storing carbon.

choices.text: The dark mud beneath a healthy salt marsh is capable of holding on to stored carbon for many hundreds of years.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the marsh's value for storing carbon as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A salt marsh, a coastal wetland flooded by the tides, supports crabs, fish, and a wide variety of wading birds.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the marsh's value for storing carbon as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to stress the marsh's value for storing carbon; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a coastal restoration project, a student took the following notes:
• The marsh lost 40% of its area since 1990.
• Volunteers replanted native grasses in 2023.
• Bird sightings rose the following spring.

The student wants to present the project's result. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: After volunteers replanted native grasses, bird sightings in the marsh rose the following spring.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to present the project's result.

choices.text: Having lost 40% of its area since 1990, the marsh had clearly declined long before volunteers ever arrived to help.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the project's result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The marsh, which had lost much of its area since 1990, is the kind of place where native grasses can be replanted.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the project's result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: In 2023, a group of dedicated volunteers who care deeply about the coast decided to replant native grasses in the marsh.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the project's result as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to present the project's result; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a freshwater turtle study, a student took the following notes:
• Females nest on the same beach for life.
• Warmer sand produces more female hatchlings.
• Researchers tagged 200 turtles over five years.

The student wants to highlight the link between temperature and sex. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Over the course of five years, the patient researchers managed to tag and track a total of two hundred turtles.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The female turtles in the study return to nest on the very same stretch of beach throughout their entire lives.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A turtle, a reptile protected by a hard shell, can live for many decades in the right freshwater habitat.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because warmer sand produces more female hatchlings, the temperature of a nesting beach can tip a turtle population toward females.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to highlight the link between temperature and sex.

explanation: The goal is to highlight the link between temperature and sex; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a wind turbine farm, a student took the following notes:
• The farm has 40 turbines on a ridge.
• Each turbine stands 120 meters tall.
• Together they power 30,000 homes.

The student wants to convey the farm's overall scale. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Standing on the ridge, each of the farm's turbines reaches an impressive height of about one hundred twenty meters.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the farm's overall scale as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A wind turbine, which turns moving air into electricity, has become a familiar sight across many rural landscapes.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the farm's overall scale as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Forty turbines, each rising 120 meters along the ridge, together generate enough electricity to power 30,000 homes.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to convey the farm's overall scale.

choices.text: The forty turbines were installed along a windswept ridge chosen for its steady and reliable seasonal breezes.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the farm's overall scale as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to convey the farm's overall scale; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a telescope upgrade, a student took the following notes:
• The old mirror was 2 meters wide.
• The new mirror is 4 meters wide.
• The upgrade doubled the telescope's resolution.

The student wants to highlight the improvement. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Astronomers rely on large telescopes like this one to gather light and study faint, distant objects across the night sky.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the improvement as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The telescope, once fitted with a 2-meter mirror, was changed at some point so that it now carries a wider mirror instead.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the improvement as the prompt asks.

choices.text: By replacing the 2-meter mirror with a 4-meter one, the upgrade doubled the telescope's resolution.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to highlight the improvement.

choices.text: The old 2-meter mirror was fairly small, which is one of several reasons astronomers decided the telescope was due for work.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the improvement as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to highlight the improvement; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching an ancient road discovery, a student took the following notes:
• Archaeologists found a stone road buried under a field.
• The road is about 2,000 years old.
• It connected two former market towns.

The student wants to introduce the discovery to readers. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Long ago, the stone road connected two market towns whose traders likely carried goods back and forth along its surface.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Archaeologists have uncovered a 2,000-year-old stone road, buried beneath a field, that once linked two market towns.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to introduce the discovery to readers.

choices.text: Archaeologists, the scientists who study the human past, often spend years carefully excavating sites buried under fields.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The road that the archaeologists studied, now roughly 2,000 years old, must have seemed quite modern when it was first built.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to introduce the discovery to readers; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a citizen science app, a student took the following notes:
• Users photograph wild plants and animals.
• Experts verify each submitted identification.
• The data helped map a rare frog's range.

The student wants to show how the app advanced real research. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: After experts verified the photos that users submitted, the app's data helped scientists map the full range of a rare frog.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to show how the app advanced real research.

choices.text: Using the app, ordinary people across the region photograph the wild plants and animals they happen to encounter.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Citizen science, in which volunteers help gather data, lets researchers collect far more observations than usual.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Every identification that a user submits through the app is later checked and verified by a qualified expert.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to show how the app advanced real research; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a meteor shower, a student took the following notes:
• The shower peaks in mid-August each year.
• Viewers may see up to 60 meteors an hour.
• It is caused by debris from an old comet.

The student wants to explain what causes the shower. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: The August shower occurs as Earth passes through the dusty trail of an old comet, where bits of debris burn up as meteors.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain what causes the shower.

choices.text: At its peak in mid-August, the shower can treat patient viewers to as many as sixty meteors every single hour.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain what causes the shower as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A meteor, often called a shooting star, is a streak of light made when a particle burns up in the atmosphere.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain what causes the shower as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Anyone hoping to catch the show should find a dark spot in mid-August and let their eyes adjust to the night sky.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain what causes the shower as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain what causes the shower; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a river otter comeback, a student took the following notes:
• Otters vanished from the river by 1970.
• Cleaner water let fish populations rebound.
• Otters returned on their own by 2015.

The student wants to link the otters' return to the cleaner water. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: By the year 1970, river otters had disappeared completely from the waterway that had once been their home.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not link the otters' return to the cleaner water as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A river otter, a sleek and playful mammal, hunts fish and crayfish along the banks of streams and rivers.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not link the otters' return to the cleaner water as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Wildlife officials were pleased to confirm that otters had come back to the river entirely on their own by 2015.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not link the otters' return to the cleaner water as the prompt asks.

choices.text: As cleaner water allowed fish populations to rebound, the otters that had vanished by 1970 returned to the river on their own by 2015.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to link the otters' return to the cleaner water.

explanation: The goal is to link the otters' return to the cleaner water; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a school orchard program, a student took the following notes:
• Students planted fruit trees on campus.
• They learn pruning and grafting by hand.
• The harvest is shared in the cafeteria.

The student wants to describe what students gain from the program. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Whatever fruit the young orchard manages to produce is gathered up and shared with everyone in the cafeteria.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe what students gain from the program as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The fruit trees that the students planted now grow in neat rows across one corner of the school's campus.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe what students gain from the program as the prompt asks.

choices.text: By planting the campus trees and tending them by hand, students learn pruning and grafting while sharing the harvest at lunch.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to describe what students gain from the program.

choices.text: An orchard, a planting of trees grown for their fruit, takes several patient years to reach full production.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe what students gain from the program as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to describe what students gain from the program; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.
